steps.text: Evaluate the performance and suitability of new sampling methods (thoroughly test).
Peer review the sampling methods.
Publish the sampling methods.

constraints.text: Peer review is required for EPA sampling methods for chemical and radiochemical parameters.

steps.text: Follow Agency Policy Directive Number FEM-2010-01 guidelines for methods of analysis developed for emergency response situations.
Review and consult sampling method guidance documents and procedures to determine the level of validation associated with the method.

preconditions.text: The analysis methods are developed for emergency response situations.

steps.text: Indicate whether the method uses a physical sampling or collection tool/technique or whether the method is a field measurement process.
Specify conditions for either type of method.
Prepare a sampling validation plan that encompasses all aspects of the validation activity and follows a standardized format similar to that shown in Appendix A.
Ensure that the new collection method is consistent with the planned analytical methods to be utilized.

constraints.text: The sampling method is closely tied to the development of the analytical methods.

steps.text: Repeat some or all aspects of the study if procedural changes are required after troubleshooting and optimizing.
Clearly describe the components of a field sampling technique or field measurement processes that are subject to validation.
Define the matrix being sampled and the purpose of the sampling effort.
Cover all aspects of the sampling event from sample collection through the delivery of the sample for analysis in the sampling methods and validation plans.
Address all aspects of the equipment/instrument's set-up, calibration, and operation, as well as sample preparation, sample cleanup, and appropriate Quality Assurance/Quality Control (QA/QC) checks.

preconditions.text: Procedural changes are required after troubleshooting and optimizing a method.
Field measurement processes are to be performed.

constraints.text: Safety concerns and procedures should be addressed in both the sampling method and the validation plan, including personal protective equipment and first aid.
Address physical hazards and chemicals that are toxic, corrosive, emit harmful or explosive vapors, or that are incompatible when mixed.

steps.text: Identify and address interferences during the development of the sampling validation plan.
Evaluate selectivity by sampling an appropriate environmental media both with and without the expected interferences.
Sample multiple environments containing varying (and measured) amounts of gravel to allow for comparison and evaluation.
Address chemical sampling devices versus physical sampling devices, as applicable, in sampling method selectivity.

preconditions.text: Soil sampling technique is impacted by the amount of gravel contained in the sample environment.

constraints.text: The choice of sampling equipment can be crucial to the task of collecting a sample appropriate for the intended use.

steps.text: Consider the uncertainties involved with the actual analytical measurement of the sample, including analytical instrumental sensitivity, instrument calibration, analytical duplicate analysis, spike sample analysis, method bias, and matrix interferences.

Consider the uncertainties involved in collection of the sample in the field, including taking an adequate number of field samples, spatial homogeneity, adequate preservation of samples, and sampling equipment and techniques.

preconditions.text: Determine the actual use of the measurement, as the depth of uncertainty analysis depends on whether it is for general understanding or regulatory/inspection purposes.

steps.text: Plan how in-depth uncertainty is to be determined before samples are collected in the field.
Calibrate, maintain, and standardize or certify all instruments used to measure or analyze samples in the field.
Certify and/or calibrate equipment used to obtain sample volumetric and/or weight measurements.

preconditions.text: Data quality objectives (DQOs) must be developed for each environmental site.

constraints.text: Instruments must be calibrated following procedures in the manufacturer's users' manual, performed in the laboratory, or as defined by the method.
Instruments require routine maintenance and at least annual standardization or certification.
Equipment calibration must use prescriptive or performance-based specifications to ensure quantitative accuracy.

steps.text: Take into account the routine sample size typically collected and submitted to the laboratory during sampling method validation studies.

Discuss the difference between collecting large numbers of potentially non-representative, heterogeneous samples using limited sampling mass or volume and collecting less of larger well-mixed samples in the validation study.
Establish and verify the validity of a sampling method by field studies.
Provide documentation of successful completion of field studies in the sampling method validation report.
Prepare a sampling method validation report suitable for placing in the public docket.
Address the sampling method validation topics outlined in this guidance in the validation report.

constraints.text: General and specific Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and good record keeping must be maintained as an essential part of a validated sampling method.

steps.text: Prepare a sampling method validation report suitable for placing in the public docket.

constraints.text: The report must address the information contained in Table 1 (including Summary, Introduction, and Methodology).

The report should provide background information on sampling method development, a description of the actual sampling method validation techniques, any changes made to the sampling method as a result of the validation studies, and any recommendations for future work.

steps.text: Include the range of analyte levels for which the method is claimed to be suitable, if field measurement is to be performed.
Include a description of any known advantages and limitations of the sampling method.
Include a list of the sampling equipment and supplies used to perform the sampling.
Include a description of how the method and analytical parameters chosen meet the data quality objectives for the specific application.
Include a discussion of how selectivity was evaluated through the sampling of appropriate environmental media both with and without expected interferences.
Include a statement describing the decision of how in-depth uncertainty was determined and what planning took place in advance of samples being collected in the field.

Include a statement focusing on the equipment that is used to measure or analyze samples in the field and how the required calibration, routine maintenance, and annual standardization are accomplished.

Include a discussion of how systematic errors were accounted for in the sampling plan through measurements of reasonably well known composition material, results comparisons to alternative methods or procedures, or using analyzed spikes.
Include a discussion of sampling method performance after minor changes are experienced in operating or environmental conditions.
Include a discussion of preserving the collected samples’ physical and chemical properties and security from the collection site to the analytical laboratory.

preconditions.text: Field measurement is to be performed

steps.text: Include a discussion of preserving the collected samples’ physical and chemical properties and security from the collection site to the analytical laboratory.
Include a discussion of how the sampling method determined the appropriate quantity of sample to be taken to support analysis.
Provide a discussion of how limits derived from mathematical definitions or statistical models were verified.
Discuss how detection limit, as it applies to instrument detection capability, was addressed in the method.
Include a discussion of the number or items or the quantity constituting a sample, as appropriate for the method, whether the samples are composites or grab.
Address safety concerns and procedures in the method validation report.
Provide a discussion of the defined quality control/quality assurance (QA/QC) checks and guidances.
Discuss method development.
Discuss results of the validation testing and an evaluation including comparison with the reference substances and preparations, acceptance criteria and recommendations.
Note any changes made to the sampling method as a result of the validation studies.
Discuss formal acceptance/rejection of work.
Provide recommendations for future work.

constraints.text: Include personal protective equipment and first aid in safety considerations.
Address physical hazards and chemicals that are toxic, corrosive, emit harmful or explosive vapors, or that are incompatible when mixed.

steps.text: Note any changes made to the sampling method as a result of the validation studies.
Discuss formal acceptance/rejection of work.
Provide recommendations for future work.
Peer review EPA sampling methods for chemical and radiochemical parameters before publication.

constraints.text: Peer review must be conducted in accordance with the most current version of the EPA Science Technology Policy Council’s Peer Review Handbook or a documented alternate procedure that ensures robustness and technical viability.

steps.text: Conduct validation planning as part of initial planning (e.g., directed planning process).
Integrate the contributions and requirements of all stakeholders and present this information in a clear, concise format in the validation plan.
Include the sampling validation plan as a section within the plan or as a stand-alone document attached as an appendix.
Address the required information in the sampling validation plan, including Purpose of Validation and Sampling Procedure (main principle, test procedures, and test conditions).
Communicate the information and documentation identified in the validation plans to the laboratory as part of the Statement of Work (SOW).
